one_line_takeaway: WaveGuard embeds a deepfake-detection watermark in DT-CWT chrominance sub-bands and reads it with a dual robust/semi-robust decoder plus a graph-based structural-consistency loss, achieving best-in-class imperceptibility (PSNR 47.5/SSIM 0.996) and near-zero tracing BER under face-swap/reenactment where prior watermarkers degrade by 2-30%.
key_finding: On CelebA-HQ, WaveGuard reports the best imperceptibility of all baselines (128x128, 30-bit: PSNR 47.5121 / SSIM 0.9957 vs. next-best LampMark 44.6251 / 0.9921; 256x256, 128-bit: 43.2530 / 0.9909 vs. LampMark 42.7825 / 0.9869). Under deepfake forgery the Tracer keeps BER near zero where baselines fail catastrophically: at 128x128 the Tracer's average deepfake BER is 0.0266% vs. SepMark 2.1313%, ARWGAN 25.44%, CIN 33.03%; at 256x256 it is 0.0047% vs. SepMark 4.7734%, FaceSigns 18.44%, ARWGAN 23.64%. Common-distortion BER is essentially zero (128x128 Tracer avg 0.0067%, Detector 0.0089%). The Detector holds a large real-vs-fake separation margin: at 256x256 its deepfake BER stays ~44.5% while its common-distortion BER is ~0.0025%. Cross-dataset transfer (LFW, FaceForensics++, DFDC, Celeb-DF v2) shows near-identical BER (deltas < 0.02% vs. CelebA-HQ). Model is 15.5M params, 13.5 GFLOPs and 70.9 ms/image at 256x256. Ablations confirm each component matters: removing DT-CWT frequency embedding (Case3) raises Tracer deepfake avg BER from 0.0266% to 0.1759%; removing SC-GNN (Case4) drops PSNR/SSIM to 42.9015/0.9417.
core_contribution: WaveGuard, a proactive watermarking framework for unified deepfake source tracing and detection. Its distinguishing design (vs. cited spatial-domain GAN/VAE watermarkers) is: (1) embedding the watermark into DT-CWT high-frequency directional sub-bands of the image's U (chrominance) channel rather than pixel space, exploiting DT-CWT translation invariance and directional selectivity; (2) a 'joint sub-band' redundancy scheme where the watermark is embedded only in sub-bands 1 and 3 but propagates into sub-bands 4 and 6, which a separable decoder pair exploits: a robust DensTracer reads sub-bands {1,3,4,6} to recover the mark under any distortion (target Wt=Wo), while a semi-robust DensDetector reads only {1,3} so its output stays correct under benign distortions but collapses toward random (~50% BER) under deepfake forgery, yielding a detection signal; (3) a Structural Consistency Graph Neural Network (SC-GNN) that turns the original and watermarked U-channels into 4-neighborhood pixel graphs, passes them through a 2-layer GCN (16-dim then scalar, global average pooling), and minimizes the MSE between graph-level embeddings as an imperceptibility/structural-consistency loss. The network is an end-to-end dense-connection encoder-decoder with two attention modules (Tanh-modified CBAM 'TSEAM' applied globally and parameter-free SIMAM after each DenseBlock).
threat_model: Proactive-defense setting: the defender watermarks a genuine face image before release. Adversary applies either (a) benign 'common' distortions (JPEG, resize, Gaussian/median blur, dropout, salt-pepper, Gaussian noise) that should preserve the mark, or (b) 'malicious' deepfake manipulations that regenerate the face -- face swap (SimSwap, Uniface, CSCS, HifiFace, FSRT) and face reenactment (GANimation, StarGAN). Goal: after distortion, the Tracer must still recover the identity/source watermark (traceability), while the Detector's watermark should survive benign edits but break under deepfake regeneration (tamper/forgery detection). The paper does NOT evaluate an adaptive, watermark-aware attacker that knows the DT-CWT sub-band scheme and tries to preserve or forge the mark; deepfake models are off-the-shelf pretrained and not optimized against the watermark.
limitations: The authors themselves note the watermark is embedded only in the chrominance (U) channel, leaving it unprotected against color-targeting distortions. The semi-robust Detector does NOT reach the ideal 50% BER under deepfakes (it sits ~46.78% at 128x128 and ~44.51% at 256x256) and is actually slightly further from 50% than SepMark's Detector (48.39%/46.29%); the paper defends this by arguing the real-vs-fake *gap* matters more than the absolute value, since its near-zero common-distortion BER widens the separation margin -- a design tradeoff, not a clean 50% collapse. Evaluation is confined to frontal-ish face datasets and off-the-shelf deepfake generators; there is no adaptive/white-box attacker aware of the DT-CWT joint-sub-band scheme, no watermark-removal attack, and no purely non-face imagery. Training is short (10 epochs) and message lengths are fixed per resolution. The claimed 'detection' is watermark-degradation-based, so it cannot flag forgeries of images that were never watermarked (an inherent proactive-forensics constraint).

datasets_used: CelebA-HQ (30,000 images, 6,217 identities; train 24,183 / val 2,993 / test 2,824) -- primary train/test
LFW (13,233 unconstrained images, 5,749 identities) -- out-of-distribution generalization
Face-swap generators used as malicious distortions: SimSwap, Uniface, CSCS, HifiFace, FSRT
Face-reenactment generators used as malicious distortions: GANimation, StarGAN
quantitative_results: Imperceptibility 128x128/30-bit: Ours PSNR 47.5121, SSIM 0.9957 (best; LampMark 44.6251/0.9921, EditGuard 38.1664/0.9796)
128x128 common-distortion avg BER: Tracer 0.0067%, Detector 0.0089% (SepMark Tracer 0.4176%/Detector 0.1610%)
128x128 deepfake avg BER: Tracer 0.0266%, Detector 46.7848% (SepMark Tracer 2.1313%/Detector 48.3893%)
256x256 deepfake avg BER: Tracer 0.0047%, Detector 44.5066% (SepMark Tracer 4.7734%/Detector 46.2854%; ARWGAN Tracer 23.6376%)
Worst-case single deepfake for Tracer (128x128): SimSwap 0.1637%, FSRT 0.0785%
Cross-dataset 128x128 PSNR ~46.27-47.67, SSIM ~0.9945-0.9957; common-BER deltas vs CelebA-HQ all < 0.004%
Complexity: 15.5M params; 3.38 GFLOPs @128x128 (74.0 ms/img), 13.5 GFLOPs @256x256 (70.9 ms/img) on RTX 4090
Ablation (128x128 Tracer deepfake avg BER): full 0.0266%; no joint-subband/Case1 0.0389%; no frequency-domain/Case3 0.1759%; no SC-GNN/Case4 0.0532% (PSNR 42.9015, SSIM 0.9417)
baselines_compared: SepMark (proactive separable watermark -- closest competitor, both resolutions)
LampMark (training-free landmark-perceptual proactive watermark, both resolutions)
